consent.comment: AdOpt consent platform — ID is language-independent

popupClose.comment: Subscription/offers popup close button — data-testid is language-independent

hints: The calendar date picker shows two months side by side. Each month is inside a container with class 'calendar'. The first .calendar is the left month, the second .calendar (nth(1)) is the right month.
Calendar navigation arrows (ChevronRightIcon) are SVG elements with role='button' directly — not inside a <button> tag. Use .calendar [data-testid='ChevronRightIcon'] to scope to calendar only.

Calendar days are
  • N
    • . Days from adjacent months have aria-hidden='true' and must be excluded. ALWAYS use: locatorMethod 'locator', locatorArg ':nth-match(.calendar, 1) [data-testid="default-day"][aria-hidden="false"]', locatorOptions: { hasText: 'N' }, useFirst: true — where N is the day number from the step. For the second/right month use ':nth-match(.calendar, 2) [data-testid="default-day"][aria-hidden="false"]'. IMPORTANT: never use .calendar:nth-of-type() or .calendar:first-of-type — those CSS pseudo-classes work on tag type, not class name, and will select wrong elements. Always use :nth-match(.calendar, N) which is the correct Playwright syntax for nth element with a given class. Never omit hasText and never omit aria-hidden filter. This selector applies equally to check-in and check-out day selection — both use data-testid='default-day', never 'disabled'.

The search autocomplete dropdown uses placeholder '¿A dónde vas a viajar?' in Spanish and 'Where are you going?' in English. Each suggestion shows a city name and a subtitle — use only the city name (e.g. 'Barcelona') to locate the suggestion, never the full subtitle text. Prefer getByText with the city name and useFirst: true. Do NOT use getByRole('option') as suggestion items may not carry that role.
The check-in / check-out fields are readonly inputs with id='checkinrooms' and id='checkoutrooms'. They require forceClick to open the date picker.

The room occupancy modal (opened via the '1 habitación · 2 adultos' or 'Huéspedes' input with data-testid='input-paxes') has counter rows for adultos and niños. Each row has - and + buttons with data-testid='RemoveIcon' and data-testid='AddIcon'. When adding/removing ADULTS, use useNth: 0. When adding/removing CHILDREN (niños), use useNth: 1. Example for adding a child: locatorMethod 'locator', locatorArg 'button:has([data-testid="AddIcon"])', useNth: 1. The children's age selector is a CUSTOM DROPDOWN — it is NOT a native <select>. Flow: (1) click [data-testid='age-selector-btn'] to open the list, (2) click the <li> inside [class*='AgesList'] that matches the desired age text. Steps: first step clicks 'age-selector-btn', second step uses locatorMethod 'locator', locatorArg '[class*="AgesList"] li', locatorOptions: { hasText: 'N' }, useFirst: true — where N is the exact age number. NEVER use selectOption or target a <select> element for children ages. The Save/Guardar button closes the modal.

The hotel photo gallery opens as a full-screen overlay when clicking a hotel photo. The category filter pills (Ver todas, Habitaciones, Exterior, Zonas comunes, Piscina, Comedor, Instalaciones) are in a sticky row at the top of the gallery. IMPORTANT: these filters are NOT <button> elements — they are <div> elements with a class containing 'ToggleIconBase'. The visible text includes a photo count in parentheses, e.g. 'Habitaciones (42)', 'Exterior (1)'. Always use: locatorMethod 'locator', locatorArg '[class*="ToggleIconBase"]', locatorOptions: { hasText: 'Habitaciones' } (partial match, no count needed). Use forceClick: true as they may be inside a scroll container.
